source: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2025
locator: page-6 'Key findings' + methodology page-57 (activity-based costing across 4 cost centers: detection & escalation, notification, post-breach response, lost business)
carries: breach RESPONSE cost as 3 of CODB's 4 cost centers (detection & escalation + notification + post-breach response). GLOBAL activity model, not per-sector; the per-sector response dollar split is a D-ii-b re-confirmation target.
single_form_slice: response cost centers: detection & escalation + notification + post-breach response (NOT lost-business)

source: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2025
locator: methodology page-57 (lost-business cost center, one of the 4 activity-based cost centers) + page-41 (86% of orgs report operational disruption)
carries: CODB 'lost-business' cost center — business disruption + churn. GLOBAL; per-sector split is a D-ii-b target.
single_form_slice: lost-business cost center (blends productivity/business-interruption + reputation churn; use for productivity only, reputation must come from a distinct source)

locator: Figure A3, p. 35 (Appendix — Loss magnitude summary statistics by sector; p50 + p95 per sector)
carries: per-sector TOTAL loss magnitude (p50+p95). Already the Epic C aggregate anchor; here it is the productivity/BI-dominant slice of the blended sector total.
single_form_slice: sector-total loss (productivity/business-interruption-dominant); NOT additively reusable for a separate response pull — response must come from a distinct source

carries: no repo source for regulatory fines & judgments magnitude. IC3 2025 (ic3.gov, direct fraud/BEC loss) is a FRAUD-loss source, NOT regulatory fines, and IC3 explicitly excludes productivity/BI — it does not map to a single FAIR loss form and is not catalogued here. D-ii-b must research a regulatory-penalty source (e.g. HIPAA/GDPR/state-AG settlements) per sector.

no repo source for asset/equipment/data replacement magnitude. (IC3 2025 BEC/wire-fraud stolen-funds loss is a direct primary transfer loss, NOT asset replacement, and is not catalogued here.) D-ii-b must research (OT/ICS equipment-replacement studies for energy/manufacturing; data-reconstruction cost).

no clean repo source for IP / trade-secret loss magnitude (meth M2). The two existing IP-exfil seed entries cite the gap-report FORBIDDEN mis-cites (DOJ 15-1433, CISA AA22-186A) and must NOT be back-filled from. D-ii-b must research adjudicated trade-secret damages.

no standalone repo reputation-loss source. CODB 'lost-business' blends reputation into productivity (catalogued under productivity, not reusable here). D-ii-b must research a standalone churn/brand source per sector.
